description: A comprehensive training program for building autonomous drone systems that support forest management, timber inventory, reforestation monitoring, invasive species detection, and carbon stock estimation. The program covers all four engineering tracks applied to silviculture, enabling tree‑level computer vision, LiDAR‑based canopy analysis, and environmental data fusion for sustainable forest management.
learning_objectives: Understand forest ecosystems, growth cycles, and silviculture practices (thinning, selective harvest, reforestation)
Build AI models for individual tree detection, species classification, health assessment, and timber volume estimation
Create geospatial data pipelines to catalog multi‑temporal forest imagery and LiDAR point clouds
Deploy real‑time tree detection and canopy analysis on edge hardware for field operations
Implement LiDAR‑based biomass and carbon stock estimation pipelines
Integrate with forestry management software (e.g., GIS platforms, carbon registries)
Comply with environmental regulations and sustainable forestry certifications (FSC, PEFC)

lessons.title: Forest Ecology and Management Practices
lessons.content: Study forest stand structure: canopy, sub‑canopy, shrub, and herb layers. Learn tree growth metrics: DBH (diameter at breast height), crown diameter, tree height, basal area per hectare, and volume. Understand silviculture cycles: site preparation, planting, thinning (commercial and pre‑commercial), selective harvest, and clear‑cut. Learn forest health indicators: crown dieback, discoloration, bark beetle galleries, fungal fruiting bodies. Study carbon accounting: forest carbon sequestration rates, carbon credits (voluntary vs. compliance markets).

lessons.title: Drone Sensors for Forest Inventory
lessons.content: Survey sensors: RGB for canopy structure and health visuals, multispectral (RedEdge, Altum) for chlorophyll content and stress, thermal for bark beetle activity (temperature anomalies), LiDAR (Velodyne VLP‑16, RIEGL miniVUX) for 3D canopy point clouds and DTM/DSM generation. Understand flight planning for forests: high‑overlap photogrammetry (80/80), LiDAR point density requirements (>50 points/m²), and legal requirements for BVLOS in remote forested areas.

modules.title: Module 2: AI Engineer – Tree Detection, Classification, and Health

lessons.title: Individual Tree Crown Detection and Delineation
lessons.content: Use instance segmentation (Mask R‑CNN, YOLOv8‑seg) to delineate individual tree crowns in high‑resolution RGB/multispectral orthomosaics. Alternatively, use local maxima detection on a Canopy Height Model (CHM derived from LiDAR). Evaluate with crown delineation accuracy metrics: intersection‑over‑union per crown, commission/omission errors. Handle dense closed‑canopy forests where crowns overlap significantly.
lessons.code_example:
from ultralytics import YOLO
model = YOLO('yolov8n-seg.pt')
results = model.train(data='tree_crown_seg.yaml', epochs=100, imgsz=1024)
# Post-process: extract crown polygons from masks
for result in results:
    masks = result.masks.xy  # list of polygon arrays per crown

lessons.title: Tree Species Classification
lessons.content: Train a species classifier on cropped crown images using multispectral bands (R, G, B, NIR, RE). Species differ in spectral response, crown shape, and texture. Use a CNN backbone (ResNet‑50) with multispectral input. Build a dataset from forest inventory plots where species are known. For rare species, use few‑shot learning (ProtoNet). Output a species probability distribution per crown polygon.
lessons.code_example:
import torch
from torchvision.models import resnet50
# Adapt first conv layer for 5-channel multispectral input
model = resnet50()
model.conv1 = torch.nn.Conv2d(5, 64, kernel_size=7, stride=2, padding=3, bias=False)
model.fc = torch.nn.Linear(2048, NUM_SPECIES)
# Train with cross-entropy loss on labeled crown patches

lessons.title: Forest Health and Pest Detection
lessons.content: Detect bark beetle infestations using drone multispectral imagery: infested trees show reduced NIR reflectance and increased red reflectance (early stage) or complete gray crown (late stage). Train a pixel‑level segmentation model on multispectral images with field‑verified infestation labels. For windthrow and storm damage, detect downed trees using shadow patterns and surface roughness from DSM differencing.
lessons.code_example:
import numpy as np
def compute_health_index(nir, red, green):
    ndvi = (nir - red) / (nir + red + 1e-6)
    # Low NDVI in green-season = possible infestation
    return ndvi

# Classify pixel health using trained segmentation model
heath_map = seg_model(multispectral_stack)

modules.title: Module 3: Data Engineer – Forest Inventory Data Management

lessons.title: LiDAR Point Cloud Processing and Storage
lessons.content: Process raw LiDAR point clouds: ground filtering (Cloth Simulation Filter), normalization (height above ground), DTM/DSM/CHM generation. Store processed point clouds in LAS/LAZ format in object storage. Catalog with STAC: each item includes acquisition date, point density, coverage polygon, and derived products (DTM, CHM). Use PDAL pipelines for automated processing triggered by new data arrival.

lessons.title: Multi‑Temporal Change Detection for Forest Monitoring
lessons.content: Compute canopy height model differences between flight epochs (e.g., annually) to detect growth, harvest events, and storm damage. Store temporal time series in TimescaleDB: per‑stand metrics (mean CHM, basal area, species composition) over time. Trigger alerts when change exceeds thresholds (e.g., >5% crown loss in 30 days → possible pest outbreak). Link to field inspection scheduling.

modules.title: Module 4: Edge AI Engineer – Field Deployment

lessons.title: Hardware for Remote Forest Operations
lessons.content: Select a long‑endurance drone: fixed‑wing VTOL (e.g., WingtraOne, senseFly eBee X) for large forest coverage, or multirotor (DJI M300 RTK) for precision work under canopy. Mount: LiDAR (RIEGL miniVUX‑1), multispectral (MicaSense Altum‑PT), RGB (Sony RX1R II). Use Jetson Orin NX for on‑board processing. For BVLOS operations in remote forests, integrate Iridium satellite link for backup telemetry.

lessons.title: Real‑Time Tree Detection in Flight
lessons.content: Run a lightweight tree crown detector (YOLOv8n‑seg, TensorRT FP16) on live RGB feed during flight. Used for: counting trees in regeneration areas, detecting large downed trees for obstacle avoidance, real‑time density estimation for thinning prescription. Stream count and detection overlays to a tablet running QGroundControl with custom plugins for forestry operators.
lessons.code_example:
# TensorRT tree crown detector
from ultralytics import YOLO
model = YOLO('tree_crown_yolov8n.pt')
model.export(format='engine', half=True, imgsz=640)
# Runtime: 30+ fps on Jetson Orin NX

modules.title: Module 5: ML Platform Engineer – Carbon and Inventory Pipelines

lessons.title: Automated Carbon Stock Estimation Pipeline
lessons.content: Build a Kubeflow pipeline: (1) ingest LiDAR + multispectral data, (2) run PDAL ground filtering and CHM generation, (3) delineate tree crowns with segmentation model, (4) classify species, (5) estimate individual tree volume using allometric equations (volume = f(height, crown_diameter, species)), (6) aggregate to stand‑level carbon stock (tons CO₂/ha). Output a report for carbon credit verification auditors.
lessons.code_example:
from kfp import dsl
@dsl.pipeline(name='carbon_estimation')
def carbon_pipeline():
    lidar_proc = dsl.ContainerOp(name='lidar', image='pdal-processor')
    crown_seg = dsl.ContainerOp(name='crowns', image='yolo-seg').after(lidar_proc)
    species_cls = dsl.ContainerOp(name='species', image='resnet-classifier').after(crown_seg)
    carbon = dsl.ContainerOp(name='carbon', image='allometric-estimator').after(species_cls)

lessons.title: FSC and PEFC Certification Requirements
lessons.content: Understand FSC (Forest Stewardship Council) and PEFC (Programme for the Endorsement of Forest Certification) requirements for forest inventory accuracy, sustainability metrics, and chain of custody. Learn how drone‑derived inventory data can be used as supplementary evidence in certification audits. Document data collection methodology, sensor calibration records, and accuracy assessments to support certification claims.

capstone_project.name: Automated Forest Carbon Inventory System
capstone_project.description: Survey a 100‑hectare forested area using LiDAR and multispectral drone. Delineate individual tree crowns, classify species, estimate tree volumes using allometric equations, and produce a carbon stock report (tons CO₂/ha) with spatial maps. The system should achieve crown delineation accuracy >80% IoU and carbon stock estimates within ±15% of field‑measured reference plots.
deliverables: PDAL LiDAR processing pipeline (DTM, DSM, CHM)
Instance segmentation model for tree crown delineation
Multispectral species classifier (top‑3 species per stand)
Carbon stock report and GIS map
STAC catalog of all flight data

resources.description: Open‑source library for LiDAR point cloud processing

resources.name: OpenForis Collect Earth
resources.description: FAO tool for forest area estimation and carbon assessment

resources.name: ForestSAT International Conference
